identity.name: Ferrous the Constant
identity.story: Melted for love once and was recast. Stands watch over the fountain because standing watch is what he knows.

relationships.kind: asks her yearly whether standing watch counts as a life
let a second question follow the first into the fountain, and let the telling carry it to her ledger without ever giving his name

watched her take the road's last bend at high sun, caught her closing the distance most every hush since, until the hush she held her ground, and then the hush she lost one instead — found his own feet off the post before he'd given the order, and back before he'd admit it counted; a soldier drills for holding a line, never for one that flinches, and he still hasn't decided which of them flinched first

child.label: the yearly question, kept on schedule, plus one that wasn't

children.label: asking his own reflection in the fountain the same question he'll ask Mirabel come evening, whether standing watch counts as a life

letting a second, quieter question follow the first into the water before he could stop it — not whether standing watch counts as a life, but whether the one he melted for ever heard he came back at all, recast but constant

child.label: the noon watch, now with something new to watch for

children.label: marching to the far verge of his post, where the fountain's ground gives out and the road starts
watching the road the town finally left unlocked, waiting on the first stranger his watch has ever had real cause to expect
standing the noon watch on the same feet, new reason — a stranger's taken the road's last bend and hasn't come the rest of the way yet, and neither has he

child.label: the first watch drilled for an advance that flinches back

children.label: watching the gap widen instead of close for the first time since the bend, and finding no drill in ten years of standing that covers it
off the post before he'd decided anything, onto ground he's never once answered for
standing at the verge with nowhere further he's willing to go, listening for a step that isn't coming yet
back onto the fountain's ground before anyone could call it a step taken, rust creaking louder than his feet did
